NEWS & TRENDS

 ROBINHOOD BIDS TO BECOME SUPER APP FOR FAMILY FINANCE (3 MINUTE READ)
[5] 

 Robinhood is expanding its platform with new tools designed to help
households manage investments and finances collectively. A new family
hub will allow users to view and manage accounts across family members
with customizable permissions, while custodial accounts let parents
invest on behalf of children with assets transferring to them when
they reach adulthood. The company is also adding recurring investments
and a gifting feature that lets family and friends contribute to a
child's portfolio, signaling Robinhood's push to become a
multi-generational financial platform. 

 AWS LEAPS INTO AGENTIC PAYMENTS (4 MINUTE READ) [6] 

 Amazon Web Services is building infrastructure that allows AI agents
to execute payments and transactions on behalf of users, aiming to
remove the final friction in automated commerce. The cloud giant is
working with Visa to create network-agnostic payment workflows so
developers can build AI-driven shopping agents that operate securely
across existing payment rails. If successful, the effort could enable
a new model of “agentic commerce,” where AI assistants handle
purchasing decisions and payments automatically, backed by large-scale
cloud infrastructure and fraud detection systems. 

 KALSHI ADDS CASH APP PAY TO FUND PREDICTION MARKET ACCOUNTS (1 MINUTE
READ) [7] 

 Cash App Pay is now available as a payment option on Kalshi's
prediction market platform, allowing users to fund trading accounts
directly from their Cash App balances. The integration connects Kalshi
to Cash App's 59 million monthly active users and simplifies account
funding through a native payment flow. 

 EXPERIAN AND OPENAI LAUNCH THE UK'S FIRST CREDIT SCORE APP INSIDE
CHATGPT (3 MINUTE READ) [8] 

 Experian has introduced a credit score comparison tool that runs
directly inside ChatGPT that allows users to see how their score
compares across postcode areas and age groups using aggregated,
anonymized data. The app provides local benchmarks and a
conversational interface designed to make financial information easier
to understand, while also guiding users to sign up to view their
personal Experian score. The move reflects Experian's strategy to
reach younger consumers through AI platforms, particularly as many
people aged 18–34 use ChatGPT but have never checked their credit
score. 

🚀 

DEEP DIVES & REPORTS

 AI IS ACCELERATING GLOBAL FRAUD LOSSES, WHICH REACHED $579 BILLION IN
2025 (2 MINUTE READ) [9] 

 A Nasdaq Verafin report estimates global fraud and scam losses
reached $579.4 billion in 2025, up 9.2% from 2023, with much of the
increase tied to criminals using AI tools. AI-driven scams accounted
for $14.3 billion in losses and improved fraud tactics such as more
convincing phishing messages and automated victim interactions.
Financial institutions are also deploying AI to detect suspicious
patterns and stop fraudulent payments, creating an escalating arms
race between attackers and defenders. 

 BUY NOW PAY LATER USAGE SURGES AS NEW PRODUCTS PROLIFERATE, JD POWER
FINDS (3 MINUTE READ) [10] 

 Installment-based payment services are becoming a routine spending
tool for Americans, with 37% of consumers using Buy Now Pay Later in
the past 90 days. While fintech providers still dominate usage,
traditional banks are outperforming them on customer satisfaction,
signaling an opportunity for banks to capture share by embedding
installment options directly into credit cards and checkout
experiences. The data also shows most purchases are repaid using the
classic “pay in four” model and are typically funded through debit
cards. 

 ANALYSIS: ROBOT MONEY AND THE FINANCIAL SYSTEM FOR AI (15 MINUTE
READ) [11]

tasks like summaries to eventually running full workflows, employees,
